description: Samovar Organic Masala Chai is hand blended in small batches using only premium, organic, cinnamon, cardamom pods, whole cloves, cracked black pepper, shredded ginger, and wild-crafted black tea. Our Organic Masala Chai blend dissipates mental fog and cloudy mornings for you just like it is done for us in San Francisco for nearly twenty years. Energy, focus, and full bodied flavor, this chai delivers pure joy in every inhalation. From your stovetop to your mug, our proprietary blend of spicy, sweet, and floral notes will fill your kitchen and please your family. (Best when sipped from your very own hand made chai mug .) Our chai blend holds up perfectly to just hot water and a mindful moment of your time. Or take a bit longer to simmer it with milk and cane sugar on your stove for a special treat, just like we do at Samovar. However you like your morning chai, it is the natural energy drink of billions of happy morning people worldwide, for hundreds of years. Having stood the test of time, masala chai delivers pure energy, mental clarity, and a natural mood boost in every single sip. Our fresh chai blend is perfect all day long. In the morning. In the afternoon. And even in the evening. The spices are whole, and sourced in small fresh batches from farmers practicing sustainably. And the wildcrafted organic Yunnan black tea in the blend has 75% of the caffeine of coffee, but will never give you the jitters of coffee, or the midday caffeine crash. All day chai energy ? Yes. Samovar masala chai fuels your tank with all day energy. Spicy, creamy, naturally sweet. The floral notes of cardamom, the zing of ginger, and the natural sweetness of cinnamon bind together in your mug and transport you to to that special place - pure tea joy. Features Blended using 100% organic ingredients Made in small batches by hand Spices and tea cure for minimum 2 months to imbue deep, complex flavor Full flavor great steeped with milk, or water How To Brew Masala Chai the Samovar Way (Serve

A traditional breakfast tea is a blend of black teas intended to stand up to a hearty morning meal. You’ll find ours rich and deep, with a lively balance of astringency and natural sweetness. Excellent with milk and sugar, this daily brew is a perfect energy-boosting alternative to coffee. PROCESSING NOTES — Fully oxidized, wild crafted Mengku broad leaf black tea INGREDIENTS — Organic black tea CAFFEINE — High ORIGIN — Yunnan, China HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

Toasted brown rice is blended with steamed green tea and stone-ground matcha green tea powder to make an extremely full-bodied, deep, creamy and sweet Japanese everyday green tea with extra caffeine. The toasted rice imparts sweet nuttiness, while the steamed sencha lends clean vegetal notes. This is a great introduction to Japanese green tea that’s easy to brew and pairs well with any meal. PROCESSING NOTES — Toasted brown rice is blended with steamed green tea and stone-ground matcha green tea powder. INGREDIENTS — Organic toasted brown rice, organic green tea, organic matcha powder CAFFEINE — Medium ORIGIN — Kagoshima, Japan HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

Our Earl Grey tea is harvested from ancient trees by the indigenous Dai tribes in the jungles of Yunnan. After harvesting, the leaves are fully oxidized, eliciting notes of caramel and pipe tobacco. They are then blended with bergamot to create the signature Earl Grey citrus aroma and flavor. PROCESSING NOTES — Wild crafted in the jungles of Yunnan, then fully oxidized, and blended with bergamot INGREDIENTS — Organic Chinese black tea, bergamot oil CAFFEINE — High ORIGIN — Yunnan Province, China HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

We wanted a delicious turmeric blend that captured the health benefits of turmeric as well as the delicious and complex qualities of ginger and citrus. Perfect for soothing frayed nerves and warming your belly. PROCESSING NOTES — Hand-blended in the U.S. and cured for up to six months INGREDIENTS — Organic ginger, organic orange peel, organic licorice root, organic turmeric, organic lemongrass, organic lemon oil CAFFEINE — Caffeine-Free ORIGIN — Hand-blended in the US HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

Our California Persian starts with traditional Persian Farudi tea, a blend of black tea and rose petals, and then adds jasmine, orange, cardamom and a hint of bergamot. Great with milk and sugar, this tea features high caffeine in a full-bodied floral infusion. PROCESSING NOTES — Hand blended tea, spices, and flowers in the US INGREDIENTS — Organic Chinese black tea, organic green tea, organic jasmine, organic rose petals, organic orange peel, organic cardamom, organic bergamot oil, natural rose flavor CAFFEINE — High ORIGIN — China, International HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

Delicious hot or iced, this creamy, sweet green tea blend makes green tea a liquid dessert. PROCESSING NOTES — Matcha green tea powder hand-blended with sugar INGREDIENTS — Shade grown, stone-milled green tea powder, cane sugar CAFFEINE — Medium ORIGIN — Japan HOW TO BREW Cold: Whisk 2 tbsp. Matcha Latte mix into 10 oz milk. Add ice. Hot: Bring 10 oz milk to a simmer. Whisk in 2 tbsp. Matcha Latte mix.

Jasmine is said to instill feelings of well-being and optimism, and to gently soothe anxiety. To produce this classic tea, jasmine blossoms are picked at their peak for sweetness, blended with fired Chinese green tea, then removed, imparting only their aroma. The result is uplifting caffeine, subtle sweetness, vegetal and nutty flavors, and an overall great mood boost. Perfect iced or hot. PROCESSING NOTES — Jasmine blossoms picked at their peak (for sweetness) are blended with fired Chinese green tea and then removed – imparting only their aroma. INGREDIENTS — Organic green tea with jasmine CAFFEINE — Low ORIGIN — Hubei, China Check out our Jasmine Iced Tea Recipe > HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 2-3 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

Custom blended for His Holiness The Dalai Lama. A decaf masala chai-style brew with a silken, malty mouth-feel and natural spicy sweetness that lingers. Rooibos imparts a natural astringency similar to that of black tea, without the caffeine. PROCESSING NOTES — Hand-blended spices and rooibos, cured for up to six months. INGREDIENTS — Organic rooibos, organic ginger, organic cinnamon, organic licorice root, organic clove, organic black pepper CAFFEINE — Caffeine-Free ORIGIN — Hand-blended in the US HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

Mint and chocolate make a delightful pairing in this herbal blend. Lavender provides the perfect middle tones to balance the flavor and aroma. Earthy, sweet, bright and intense. PROCESSING NOTES — Hand blended and then cured for up to six months to allow for a slow melding of flavors and aromas. INGREDIENTS — Organic cocoa shells, organic peppermint, organic lavender CAFFEINE — Caffeine-Free ORIGIN — Ecuadorian cocoa shells, American peppermint, French lavender HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

Classically soothing chamomile flowers with a citrus and spearmint kick. PROCESSING NOTES — Hand-blended chamomile flowers, lemon myrtle, lemongrass, and spearmint INGREDIENTS — Organic chamomile, organic lemongrass, organic lemon myrtle, organic spearmint CAFFEINE — Caffeine-Free ORIGIN — Hand-blended in the US HOW TO BREW Add 2 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

A fruity, tart and refreshing herbal blend that is bright and energizing. Makes a great iced tea. PROCESSING NOTES — Hand blended rooibos, hibiscus, lemongrass, rose hips, licorice root, cured for up to six months. INGREDIENTS — Organic rooibos, organic hibiscus, organic lemongrass, organic rose hips, organic licorice root, natural pineapple and passion fruit flavors CAFFEINE — Caffeine-Free ORIGIN — Hand-blended in the US HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

A silken smooth body and accents of chocolate and coconut make for a decadent brew. Enjoy it with a hunk of dark chocolate. With a mild caffeine buzz, this tea offers a great alternative to coffee. PROCESSING NOTES — Aged pu-erh tea, hand blended in the US INGREDIENTS — Organic pu-erh tea, organic cocoa shells, organic yerba mate, organic roasted dandelion root, organic coconut flakes, organic vanilla bean CAFFEINE — Medium ORIGIN — Yunnan, China, International HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

Uniquely complex and multi-layered, Spearmint Sage is our take on classic mint tea. Our blend delivers bright and uplifting minty-flavor balanced with savory sage and lavender. Energizing, yet caffeine-free; refreshing, minty zing with subtlety and depth. PROCESSING NOTES — Hand blended and cured for up to six months INGREDIENTS — Organic peppermint, organic lemongrass, organic lavender, organic orange peel, organic spearmint, organic white sage CAFFEINE — Caffeine-Free ORIGIN — Peppermint (Yakima Valley Washington), lemongrass (Retalhuleu, Guatemala),lavender (USA), orange peel (USA), spearmint (Yakima Valley Washington), white sage (USA) HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

The quality of matcha is determined by the time and care taken in selecting and processing the leaves. The more uniform the leaves and size of particles, the more resonant and remarkable the brew. This mid-level matcha is slightly sweeter and has a smoother mouthfeel than the Mainichi, with notes of steamed rice, sweet potato and green hay. PROCESSING NOTES — Shading the tea bushes allows only 10% of the sunlight to enter the slowly growing plants, increasing chlorophyll concentration, sweetness, and caffeine. Hand-scissoring (not machine), deveining the leaves, and granite-milling (not ceramic) yield a deep, creamy, extremely fine vivid green matcha. INGREDIENTS — Organic Stone-milled, shade grown, Japanese green tea powder CAFFEINE — Medium-High ORIGIN — Japan HOW TO BREW Sift 1 TSP into matcha bowl. Add 6 oz hot water. Whisk until small tight bubbles are formed.

The perfect introduction to pu-erh. Mellow, woodsy notes are rounded out by a balanced citrus and ginger spice kick, and a nice caffeine boost. PROCESSING NOTES — Aged in China, hand blended in the US. INGREDIENTS — Organic pu-erh tea, organic ginger, organic orange peel, blood orange and grapefruit oil CAFFEINE — Medium ORIGIN — Yunnan, China HOW TO BREW Add 2 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

Traditional Moroccan or Maghrebi mint tea is made by brewing green tea with spearmint leaves and sugar. Our version is more complex in flavor, with hints of ginger and cardamom, while the black pepper adds mellow warmth. The green tea base adds just a bit of briskness. PROCESSING NOTES — Hand blended herbs, spices and green tea and cured for up to 6 months. INGREDIENTS — Organic ginger, organic peppermint, organic licorice root, organic green tea, organic cardamom, organic fennel, organic black pepper, organic clove CAFFEINE — Low ORIGIN — International HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

Wild-crafted from 90-foot trees in the jungles of Southwest China, this tea is a traditional wedding gift and evening mood-setter. Dark, creamy, and luxurious, the flavor mellows in subtle ways over multiple infusions. PROCESSING NOTES — Aged and fermented 5+ years. Wild-crafted from the jungles of Yunnan, China INGREDIENTS — Organic pu-erh tea CAFFEINE — Medium ORIGIN — Yunnan, China HOW TO BREW Add 2 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

Also known as “Tiequanyin” this darker style rolled Taiwanese oolong is rich, sweet, deep and complex. Very smooth, with a gently astringent finish. Good for multiple infusions. PROCESSING NOTES — Hand rolled, medium oxidized INGREDIENTS — Taiwanese oolong tea CAFFEINE — Medium-high ORIGIN — Nantou, Taiwan Learn about Tie Kuan Yin, the Iron Goddess of Mercy > HOW TO BREW Add 12 oz boiling water to 1 tbs tea, steep 3 seconds, discard this infusion. Add 12 oz boiling water, steep 4 min, serve.

All our matchas are shade-grown, carefully hand-picked, deveined by hand and stone-milled, resulting in an ultra creamy, deep, sweet brew. With matcha you’re literally “eating” the tea as the fine particles float in your brew, providing health benefits as well as energizing caffeine. Mainichi (“everyday”) matcha is an everyday, approachable premium matcha, delicious hot or cold. INGREDIENTS — Organic Stone-milled, shade grown, Japanese green tea powder CAFFEINE — Medium-high ORIGIN — Japan HOW TO BREW Sift 1 TSP into matcha bowl. Add 6 oz hot water. Whisk until small tight bubbles are formed.

The name of this most popular green tea says it all. Asamushi-style first flush Japanese blended sencha with matcha. With more caffeine and mouthfeel than the average Japanese sencha, this tea is easy to drink, energizing, complex, rich, and totally addictive. Packed at the farm in small 50 gram (2 ounce) bags. Be mindful when brewing this tea to use lower temperature water to avoid scalding the leaves. PROCESSING NOTES — Matcha powder blended with first flush asamushi Japanese green tea INGREDIENTS — Organic sencha and organic matcha powder CAFFEINE — Medium-High ORIGIN — Kagoshima, Japan Learn more about Best By Dates > HOW TO BREW Add 1 level TBSP tea to brewing vessel. Cover with 2 oz. cold, filtered water. Steep 1 minute. Add 12 oz. boiling water. Steep 2 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

Just like our Earl Grey black tea, but totally decaffeinated. PROCESSING NOTES — Hand blended organic rooibos and bergamot INGREDIENTS — Organic rooibos, bergamot CAFFEINE — Caffeine-Free ORIGIN — USA HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

A rich, fertile soil and thick daily fog give this Taiwanese oolong a buttery, floral and sweetly complex flavor. Two leaves and a sliver of stem are hand-rolled to form each ball. Lightly oxidized for an oolong, with strong green notes that provide full body and balance. Good for 3 or more infusions. PROCESSING NOTES — Stem and two leaves are lightly oxidized and hand rolled. INGREDIENTS — Taiwanese oolong tea CAFFEINE — Medium-High ORIGIN — Nantou, Taiwan Learn more about oolong tea > HOW TO BREW Add 12 oz boiling water to 1 tbs tea, steep 3 seconds, discard this infusion. Add 12 oz boiling water, steep 4 min, serve.

PROCESSING NOTES — First flush, asamushi-style sencha (lightly steamed), picked early in the spring. Very elegant, succulent, and clean — a classic Japanese green tea that is pure joy. L-theanine, that naturally occurring mood booster your brain releases in yoga class, also occurs in the best Japanese green teas, which is what makes this tea taste and feel so good. Packed at the farm in small, 50 gram (2 ounce) bags. Be mindful when brewing this to use lower temperature water, to prevent scalding the leaves. INGREDIENTS — Organic Japanese green tea CAFFEINE — Low ORIGIN — Kagoshima, Japan HOW TO BREW Add 1 level TBSP tea to brewing vessel. Cover with 4 oz. cold, filtered water. Steep 2 minutes. Add 8 oz. boiling water. Steep 30 seconds. Decant and enjoy!

Fruity and bright with classic black tea astringency. Rose hips and hibiscus impart a vitamin C boost and natural tart flavor, balanced by the sweet fragrance of peach. Delicious with milk and sugar, colonial-style. PROCESSING NOTES — Fully oxidized Chinese black tea hand blended in the US with fruit and flowers. INGREDIENTS — Organic Chinese black tea, organic rose hips, organic peach pieces, organic hibiscus, natural peach flavor CAFFEINE — Medium-high ORIGIN — China, USA HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

For this seasonal favorite, we take our signature masala chai blend and add not only pumpkin pieces and sweet maple crystals, but also hearty roasted dandelion root and star anise to create a brew that is both familiar and exotic. INGREDIENTS — Organic black tea, organic cinnamon, pumpkin pieces, organic ginger root, organic maple crystals, organic roasted dandelion root, organic black pepper, organic star anise, natural pumpkin, whipped cream and caramel flavors CAFFEINE — High ORIGIN — Hand-blended in the U.S. HOW TO BREW Dissolve 3 Tbsp. sweetener in 2 cups boiling water, add 3 Tbsp chai. Boil uncovered 10 mins. Add 2 cups milk, bring to a boil, strain.

Made from whole leaves harvested in early spring. The leaves are rolled by hand into pearls before they are scented with jasmine blossoms. Soothing, uplifting, energizing, beautiful to watch unfurl. These are the finest jasmine pearls available. PROCESSING NOTES — Harvest begins at the end of April and early May. The leaf and bud groups are picked together and pan fired. The processed tea is kept until late May when the jasmine begins to bud. Then the tea is scented multiple times. After scenting, all the jasmine buds are removedand the tea is steamed to make it pliable again. Finally the tea is hand rolled into it’s famed pearl shape to seal in the flavors and aromas. INGREDIENTS — Green tea with jasmine CAFFEINE — Medium ORIGIN — Fujian, China Check out our Jasmine Iced Tea Recipe > HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

Pure chunks of golden turmeric and ginger are delicious as is, or can be used for a clean, classic Golden Milk tea base. INGREDIENTS — Organic turmeric, organic ginger CAFFEINE — Caffeine-free HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

Sweet and woodsy and caffeinated and good for many infusions. A classic everyday oolong. PROCESSING NOTES — Twisted leaf, deep oxidization INGREDIENTS — Organic Chinese oolong CAFFEINE — Medium-high ORIGIN — Wuyi Mountain, Fujian, China Learn more about oolong tea > HOW TO BREW Add 12 oz boiling water to 1 tbs tea, steep 3 seconds, discard this infusion. Add 12 oz boiling water, steep 4 min, serve.

A full, rich, creamy white tea with sweet and savory melon notes. Not your typical delicate white tea, and yet definitely sophisticated and complete enough to drink every day, all day. PROCESSING NOTES — Withered and air dried INGREDIENTS — Organic white tea CAFFEINE — Low ORIGIN — Yunnan, China HOW TO BREW Add 2 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 3 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

Tart, and fruity, with a touch of spice. Drink it in cold months with a bit of honey, or in hot months chilled over ice. PROCESSING NOTES — Hand-blended in the U.S. and cured for up to six months. INGREDIENTS — Organic blackberry leaves, organic cranberries, organic hibiscus, organic apple, organic lemon peel, organic cinnamon, organic eucalyptus, organic orange peel CAFFEINE — Caffeine-Free ORIGIN — Hand-blended in the US HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

Notes of toasted walnuts, caramel, and milk chocolate. INGREDIENTS —Japanese green tea powder, sugar CAFFEINE — High ORIGIN —Hand Blended in the USA Learn more about Best By Dates > HOW TO BREW Cold: Whisk 2 tbsp. Sweet Houjicha mix into 10 oz milk. Add ice. Hot: Bring 10 oz milk to a simmer. Whisk in 2 tbsp. Sweet Houjicha mix.

One of the most exclusive and expensive teas on the market. Difficult and time-consuming to make, this tea yields one of the highest umami flavors available and provides very high levels of L-theanine. PROCESSING NOTES — Aka “Jade Dew,” gyokuro is covered with “tana” (black nets and bamboo frames) to shade the tea, concentrate the chlorophyll, increase L-theanine and caffeine, and yield pure umami. INGREDIENTS — Shade-grown Japanese green tea CAFFEINE — Medium ORIGIN — Uji, Japan Learn more about Gyokuro Green Tea > Learn more about Best By Dates > HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP tea, cover with 2 oz cold water, steep 5 min. Enjoy this first infusion as an umami blast to the tastebuds. Then Add 10 oz hot (130º) water, and steep for 30 seconds . Enjoy right away. Re-steep these leaves 2-5 more times and look for an increase in astringency and a decrease in umami flavor.

Easily mistaken for a black tea, this is a green tea that’s been roasted for a toasty, dark, malty experience. Winter-harvested, roasted tea leaves and stems. Great as a digestif, or chilled as an iced tea. PROCESSING NOTES — Winter-harvested, roasted tea leaves and stems INGREDIENTS — Organic Japanese green tea CAFFEINE — Medium ORIGIN — Japan Learn more about Best By Dates > HOW TO BREW Add 1 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

Ancient detoxifying herbs in a modern tea blend. Earthy, creamy and spicy, like a decaf chai. Bittersweet and cleansing. PROCESSING NOTES — Hand-blended in the U.S. and cured for up to six months INGREDIENTS — Organic dandelion root, organic cinnamon, organic cocoa shells, organic ginger, organic sarsaparilla root, organic maqui berries, organic black pepper, organic cloves, organic ramón nut CAFFEINE — Caffeine-Free ORIGIN — Hand-blended in the US HOW TO BREW Add 2 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 5 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

Pure, top quality Croatian chamomile buds. Sweet nectar and gentle floral notes. INGREDIENTS — Organic Croatian chamomile CAFFEINE — Caffeine-Free ORIGIN — Croatia HOW TO BREW Add 2 TBSP to 12 oz. boiling water and steep for 4 minutes. Decant and enjoy!

A single varietal ancient tea from Phoenix Mountain in Guangdong, China. This special brew exudes extraordinarily vivid fruit and sweet honey aromas and flavor, making it a premium tea prized by tea lovers. A twisted-leaf oolong, golden phoenix is harvested from wild, individual tea trees, not farmed. The leaves are withered after picking, pan-fired over charcoal at high heat in two stages, and dried in bamboo baskets. PROCESSING NOTES — Picked from wild, individual tea trees, no farm. A twisted-leaf oolong, the leaves are withered after picking, pan fired over charcoal at high heat (140-160 degrees) in two stages, then dried in bamboo baskets in two stages, for up to 3 hours depending on moisture content and the final shape of the leaf. INGREDIENTS — Oolong tea CAFFEINE — High ORIGIN — Phoenix Mountain, Guangdong, China Learn more about oolong tea > Learn more about Best By Dates > HOW TO BREW Add 12 oz boiling water to 2 tbs tea, steep 5 seconds, discard this infusion. Add 12 oz boiling water, steep 3 min, serve.
